title: cypholophus rudis
intro: Cypholophus rudis, also known as the 'Maile', is a flowering plant species native to the tropical forests of the Pacific Islands, particularly in Samoa and Tonga. Traditionally, Cypholophus rudis has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Cypholophus rudis include the treatment of fever, colds, and respiratory issues, as well as its use as an expectorant and a digestive aid. In terms of herbal preparations, Cypholophus rudis leaves can be infused in hot water to make a tea, or they can be dried and used in infusions, decoctions, or tinctures for various medicinal uses.
